title: Trifolium schimperi
taxonomy: Trifolium schimperi is classified as a species of legume in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea or bean family. It belongs to the genus Trifolium, which includes over 200 species of clover. The species is native to the Horn of Africa and the Arabian Peninsula, where it can be found growing in grasslands and open woodlands. Within the genus Trifolium, T. schimperi is part of the section Trifoliastrum, which is characterized by its small flowers and hairy leaves. The specific classification of T. schimperi reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Fabaceae family.

intro: Trifolium schimperi, also known as Schimper's clover, is a perennial legume native to the Ethiopian Highlands. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and digestive disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. Bioactive constituents of Trifolium schimperi include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made with Trifolium schimperi include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly used in traditional Ethiopian medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
